title: In conversation with Mark Cuban
description:
(0:00) The Besties intro Mark Cuban: Voting record, working with Ross Perot's campaign in 1992

(5:43) The history of Cuban's love/hate relationship with Trump

(19:08) Trump's performance as President: what he got right and wrong

(40:46) Party nominations: Kamala vs. Trump

(49:18) Biden's performance as President: what he got right and wrong

(55:45) Should Kamala share blame for Biden's failures?

(1:07:01) International conflict, national debt, crypto regulation

(1:21:33) General sense of Kamala Harris, why she's been avoiding adversarial interviews, why Sacks supports Trump

(1:31:47) Selling a majority of his Mavericks stake, changing business landscape of the NBA, what he's working on at Cost Plus Drugs

(1:44:50) Thoughts on AI, what's next for him

(1:51:37) Relationship with Elon, re-evaluating the Twitter deal, OpenAI's new fundraise

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast features Mark Cuban, discussing his voting record, Trump's presidency, Biden's performance, and international conflict. Cuban also shares his thoughts on AI, crypto regulation, and his business ventures. The conversation covers various topics, including politics, business, and technology.

takeaways.text: Mark Cuban has a complex relationship with Trump
Kamala Harris may share blame for Biden's failures
Cuban is working on Cost Plus Drugs
Cuban has thoughts on AI and crypto regulation
Cuban sold a majority of his Mavericks stake

sentiment.notes: The conversation has a mix of positive and negative sentiments, with Cuban criticizing Trump's presidency and Biden's performance, but also sharing his thoughts on AI and business ventures.

notable_moments.description: Cuban shares thoughts on international conflict and national debt
